intro: Carex pamiroalaica, also known as Pamir sedgy sedge, is a type of perennial plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae, commonly found in mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, due to its reported health benefits of alleviating inflammation, improving gut health, and enhancing immune function. The bioactive constituents of Carex pamiroalaica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
